temporal_description: A close-up shot focuses on a man with glasses and dark hair, wearing a suit and tie. He holds a lit cigarette to his lips, and smoke drifts around his face. He looks intently at someone just out of frame to the left. In the dark background, the shoulder of another person is visible. In the top left corner, white Chinese characters are displayed. The camera cuts to a reverse close-up of a second man. This man has a mustache and is also holding a lit cigarette. He stares forward with a wide-eyed, intense expression. The background is completely black, isolating him in the frame. The same white Chinese characters remain in the top left corner. A man speaks in a low, menacing voice, his tone calm but filled with a quiet, threatening intensity. He begins with a calm, almost taunting question, "怎么样？" (How about it?). The shot returns to the first man with glasses. He continues to hold the cigarette, his expression serious and focused as he looks at the other person. The lighting is dim, highlighting his face against the dark background. The white Chinese characters are still visible in the top left corner. His voice then becomes louder and more aggressive, filled with impatience and anger as he commands, "走啊！" (Go!). The intensity escalates as he repeats the command with sharp, forceful anger, "叫你走啊，听见没有？" (I told you to go, do you hear me?). The scene is devoid of any background music or significant sound effects, making the man's threatening voice the central focus of the audio.
